fields.i_receive: every pixel your cursor touches, fed to gemini
fields.you_receive: you can say 'fix this' instead of typing

narrative_summary: Google's AI pointer deal is framed as a comically lopsided trade: Google vacuums up everything under your cursor via Gemini surveillance while you get the convenience of a two-word voice command.
rationale: Candidate 3 earns the specificity point by naming Gemini directly in the trade offer itself rather than burying it in the summary, making the surveillance joke land with a concrete target. The 'instead of typing' closer is marginally crisper than 'at a pdf' because it highlights the laziness exchange rather than just the absurdity of the medium.
story_title: Reimagining the mouse pointer for the AI era
place: inside a chatbot's context window that's almost full
hat_pair: bucket hat with a tiny aquarium built into the brim
